summary: Completed testing of Photo Minting Bug Fix (Iteration 36). The fix for 'Minting Failed' error is verified working. The bug was caused by incorrect AI vision API usage - ImageContent was being passed with data URL prefix which caused double-encoding. The fix passes raw base64 data without the prefix.

issues: Minor: Console warning about missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent

passed_tests: Backend: /api/minting/config returns correct structure with mint_cost_bl=500, scenery_types, light_types, rating_criteria
Backend: /api/minting/status returns can_mint, mints_today, daily_limit, bl_coins
Backend: /api/minting/photo/upload successfully mints photo with AI analysis
Backend: AI correctly identifies scenery_type (water, natural, manmade)
Backend: AI correctly identifies light_type (sunlight_fire, rain_snow_ice, darkness_night)
Backend: Dollar value calculated correctly ($1M-$1B range based on ratings)
Backend: BL coins deducted (500 BL per mint)
Backend: Daily limit enforced (3 mints for free tier)
Backend: Photo appears in user's collection after minting
Backend: /api/minting/photos returns user's minted photos with all AI analysis fields
Frontend: Minted Photos page loads with stats (Total Photos, Portfolio Value, Battles, Mints Today)
Frontend: Photo cards display correctly with scenery type, dollar value, power, strength/weakness badges
Frontend: Mint New dialog opens with BL Coins, Mints Today, Cost info
Frontend: File upload area present in mint dialog
Frontend: Daily limit message displayed when limit reached
critical_code_review_comments: The fix in minting_system.py line 200 correctly passes raw base64 to ImageContent without data URL prefix
The emergentintegrations library adds the data URL prefix automatically in _add_user_message
AI analysis returns all 10 rating criteria with scores in 40-95 range as expected
Dollar value calculation uses weighted average of ratings with exponential scale
seed_data_creation: Created test photo via /api/minting/photo/upload with real image from picsum.photos
context_for_next_testing_agent: Photo minting bug fix verified working. Admin user has 3 minted photos (daily limit reached). Test user also at daily limit. AI analysis correctly identifies scenery_type (water/natural/manmade), light_type (sunlight_fire/rain_snow_ice/darkness_night), and calculates dollar values in $1M-$1B range. The fix was passing raw base64 to ImageContent instead of data URL prefixed string.
